priority_1_critical.integration: Merge proactive engine's monitoring loop WITH immune system's adaptive scan loop. The immune system already has anomaly detection (8 types), vaccination, healing playbook, and rollback. The proactive engine should DELEGATE health assessment to the immune system rather than duplicating it.
Feed diagnostic machine sensor data into proactive engine trend analysis. Use interpreter layer for pattern detection, judgement layer for forensic findings, action router for healing dispatch.

Stress test results should feed directly into self-healing. When stress tests fail, the healing system should be notified immediately and attempt remediation. Stress test should also run AFTER healing to verify the fix worked.

priority_2_important.integration: Use OODA for healing decision-making. Observe: collect metrics. Orient: compare against baselines. Decide: select healing action. Act: execute. This replaces the simple if/else logic in handle_issue().
priority_2_important.data_flow: Metrics → OODA.observe() → orient() → decide() → act() → HealingAction

priority_2_important.integration: Use mirror's behavioral baselines as the NORMAL against which anomalies are detected. Mirror should observe healing patterns and suggest improvements.
Use trust scores to gate autonomous healing. High-trust actions execute automatically. Low-trust actions require governance approval. Trust should increase on successful heals, decrease on failures.
Use temporal awareness to schedule healing at optimal times. Avoid healing during peak usage. Detect time-based patterns (e.g., memory leak every 2 hours).

priority_3_enhancement.integration: Wrap healing actions in circuit breakers to prevent healing loops (healing that causes more damage). 41 named loops already exist — add healing-specific ones.
For CRITICAL healing decisions (state rollback, isolation, emergency shutdown), run through multi-model consensus before executing.
Log every healing action to telemetry. Use performance baselines for drift detection. Replay telemetry to debug failed healings.
Send notifications on critical healing events via configured channels (Slack, webhook, email).
Store every healing outcome as a learning example. Build a healing playbook that improves over time.
Broadcast healing events to connected UI clients in real-time.

additional_capabilities_beyond_12.name: immune_adaptive_scan
additional_capabilities_beyond_12.description: Delegate to GraceImmuneSystem for adaptive interval scanning with anomaly type detection (8 types: performance degradation, memory leak, resource exhaustion, cascade failure, pattern drift, security anomaly, service down, code error)

additional_capabilities_beyond_12.name: stress_test_verification
additional_capabilities_beyond_12.description: Run targeted stress tests after healing to verify the fix holds under load. Failed verification triggers re-healing with escalated strategy.

additional_capabilities_beyond_12.name: forensic_root_cause
additional_capabilities_beyond_12.description: Use diagnostic machine's 4-layer pipeline (sensors → interpreters → judgement → action) for deep forensic root cause analysis before healing.

additional_capabilities_beyond_12.name: ooda_decision_loop
additional_capabilities_beyond_12.description: Route healing decisions through OODA (observe/orient/decide/act) for structured decision-making rather than simple threshold checks.

additional_capabilities_beyond_12.name: behavioral_baseline_comparison
additional_capabilities_beyond_12.description: Use mirror self-modeling to maintain behavioral baselines and detect anomalies as deviations from normal patterns.

additional_capabilities_beyond_12.name: vaccination_proactive_hardening
additional_capabilities_beyond_12.description: After healing a recurring issue, apply the immune system's vaccination mechanism to proactively harden against recurrence.

additional_capabilities_beyond_12.name: temporal_pattern_healing
additional_capabilities_beyond_12.description: Use TimeSense to detect time-based failure patterns (e.g., memory leak every 2 hours) and schedule preemptive healing.

additional_capabilities_beyond_12.name: cascade_failure_prevention
additional_capabilities_beyond_12.description: Detect cascade failure patterns where one service failure triggers others. Isolate affected components before cascade propagates.

additional_capabilities_beyond_12.name: drift_detection_healing
additional_capabilities_beyond_12.description: Use telemetry's drift detection to identify when system behavior drifts from established baselines and trigger corrective healing.

additional_capabilities_beyond_12.name: consensus_critical_decisions
additional_capabilities_beyond_12.description: For critical/destructive healing actions (rollback, isolation, shutdown), run through multi-model consensus (Kimi + Opus) before executing.

additional_capabilities_beyond_12.name: healing_playbook_learning
additional_capabilities_beyond_12.description: Build a structured healing playbook from outcomes. Each healing attempt creates a learning example. Over time, the playbook becomes the primary decision source.

additional_capabilities_beyond_12.name: circuit_breaker_loop_prevention
additional_capabilities_beyond_12.description: Wrap all healing actions in circuit breakers to prevent healing loops where a fix causes new damage that triggers more healing.

additional_capabilities_beyond_12.name: sandbox_safe_testing
additional_capabilities_beyond_12.description: Before applying risky healing actions to production, test them in the sandbox first. Only apply if sandbox verification passes.

additional_capabilities_beyond_12.name: realtime_ui_broadcast
additional_capabilities_beyond_12.description: Broadcast all healing events, predictions, and decisions to the UI via WebSocket for real-time visibility.

additional_capabilities_beyond_12.name: notification_escalation
additional_capabilities_beyond_12.description: Send Slack/webhook/email notifications for critical healing events. Escalation ladder: log → UI → Slack → email based on severity.

additional_capabilities_beyond_12.name: self_capability_expansion
additional_capabilities_beyond_12.description: When the system encounters a problem it cannot heal, it creates a capability gap report, uses Kimi to design a healing strategy, tests it in sandbox, and if governance approves, adds it as a new capability.

additional_capabilities_beyond_12.name: code_repair_via_coding_agent
additional_capabilities_beyond_12.description: For code-level issues (broken imports, syntax errors, stub code), delegate to the unified coding agent for automated repair with genesis key tracking.

additional_capabilities_beyond_12.name: autonomous_rollback
additional_capabilities_beyond_12.description: Maintain state snapshots before healing. If healing makes things worse (verified by stress test), automatically rollback to pre-healing state.

unified_architecture.description: The Immune System should be the ORCHESTRATOR that coordinates all healing. The Proactive Engine provides the real-time monitoring loop. The Diagnostic Machine provides deep analysis. The OODA loop provides decision structure. Mirror provides baselines. Trust gates autonomy. TimeSense schedules windows. Stress tests verify fixes.
data_flow_diagram: → collects metrics (psutil, DB, Qdrant, LLM, error counts)
→ ImmuneSystem uses OODA: observe(metrics) → orient(baselines from Mirror) → decide(action) → act(heal)
→ If fail: rollback, escalate, try alternative
→ If capability gap: create gap report → Kimi designs solution → sandbox test → governance approval → add capability

implementation_priority: Phase 1 (Immediate): Integrate immune system + diagnostic machine + stress test verification
Phase 2 (Next): Add OODA decision-making + mirror baselines + trust gating + TimeSense scheduling
Phase 3 (Enhancement): Circuit breaker + consensus for critical decisions + telemetry drift detection
Phase 4 (Self-Building): Healing playbook learning + self-capability expansion + autonomous rollback + sandbox safe testing
Phase 5 (Full Autonomy): Real-time UI broadcast + notification escalation + code repair via coding agent
